figures.caption: Table 1: Organ-level performance comparison of ten retrieval configurations under Top-1 and Top-3 retrieval. Values report mean F1-macro ± s.d. across 17 organs, with 95% confidence intervals (CI). TITAN is treated as the baseline model.

Fig. 1: Comparative evaluation of whole-slide image retrieval models across 17 organs. a,b, Mean organ-averaged F1-macro across 17 organs for Top-1 (a) and Top-3 (b) retrieval. Bars denote mean ± s.d.; dots indicate individual organ scores. Significance brackets indicate two-sided paired comparisons against TITAN (ns, not significant; *p < 0.05; **p < 0.01; ***p < 0.001; n = 17 organs). c,d, Radar plots showing organ-level F1-macro for all ten retrieval configurations under Top-1 (c) and Top-3 (d) retrieval. e,f, Heatmaps of organ-specific F1-macro under Top-1 (e) and Top-3 (f) retrieval; bold values indicate the highest score within each organ. g,h, Number of organs for which each model achieved the highest F1-macro under Top-1 (g) and Top-3 (h) retrieval. [Note that Yottixel+CONCH has been shortened to Yottixel in the figure]

Fig. 2: Diagnosis-level variation in retrieval performance across ten retrieval configurations. a,b, Heat maps showing F1 scores under Top-1 retrieval (a) and Top-3 retrieval (b) across TITAN, ABMIL, patch-based Yottixel+CONCH variants (5%, 10%, 20%, 50%, 100%), Madeleine, PRISM and GigaPath. Only diagnoses for which TITAN did not achieve the highest F1 score were included (27 diagnoses under Top1; 29 diagnoses under Top-3). Rows indicate diagnoses and columns indicate models; bold values indicate the highest score within each diagnosis; higher F1 scores are shown in green and lower scores in red. c,d, Number of diagnoses for which each model ranked first by F1 score under Top-1 (c) and Top-3 (d) retrieval (ties at scores of 0 or 1 excluded). [Note that Yottixel+CONCH has been shortened to Yottixel in the figure]

Fig. 3: Diagnosis prevalence is associated with, but does not fully determine, retrieval performance. a,b, Scatter plots show diagnosis-level F1 score (y-axis) versus the number of cases for that diagnosis (x-axis) under Top-1 (a) and Top-3 (b) retrieval. Each point represents one diagnosis. Dotted reference lines indicate the Gaussian-mixture-derived case-count threshold (x) and F1 threshold (y) used to partition diagnoses into support-performance regimes for each model (values annotated within each subplot; see Methods). The case-count threshold was highly stable across models (x = 223–226 in all panels). Top-1 F1 thresholds were 0.84 (TITAN), 0.75 (ABMIL), 0.82 (Yottixel+CONCH (100%)), 0.82 (Yottixel+CONCH (50%)), 0.83 (Yottixel+CONCH (20%)), 0.79 (Madeleine), 0.77 (PRISM), 0.72 (Yottixel+CONCH (10%)), 0.73 (Yottixel+CONCH (5%)) and 0.71 (GigaPath). Top-3 F1 thresholds were 0.79, 0.77, 0.81, 0.83, 0.83, 0.80, 0.73, 0.71, 0.68 and 0.69, respectively. [Note that Yottixel+CONCH has been shortened to Yottixel in the figure]
